name: Object
comment: Base class for all classes.

classMethods.name: myTest
classMethods.comment:
This is the first method compiled.
	Can be changed when testing the compiler.

classMethods.name: new
classMethods.comment:
The basicNew method is generated by the compiler in each class
	 to instantiate ST objects from their generated JS class.

classMethods.name: fromJs:
classMethods.comment: Return new ST object containing values of argument JS object, recursively.

methods.name: toJs
methods.comment:
Converts self to a JS object.
	 Array overloads this to convert array elements to JS also, recursively.
	 So the resulting object is a JS (not ST) object
	 and is suitable for passing to JS functions, and conversion to JSON.

methods.name: toJsObject
methods.comment: Calls toJs and wraps result in a JsObject.

methods.name: toJson
methods.comment: Return a JSON string representing self.
Return true a self and arg refer to the same object.

Return true a self and arg have the same value.
	 The default is to return object equality.
	 Overload this for composed objects.

methods.name: ifNil:
methods.comment: I'm not nil, so don't evaluate block.

methods.name: perform:with:with:
methods.header: perform: methodName with: argument1 with: argument2

methods.name: perform:with:with:with:
methods.header: perform: methodName with: argument1 with: argument2 with: argument3

name: AiProviders
comment:
Encapsulates a map of providers to models.
 Communicated by the AI server to the client.

 A JSON example of the communication is:
	[	{ provider: 'OpenAI', models: [ 'gpt-4o-mini', ' gpt-4o' ] }
		{ provider: 'Deepseek', models: [ 'deepseek-chat' 'deepseek-reasoner' ] } ]

methods.name: toObject
methods.comment:
Converts self to a ST object that can be converted to JSON.
	 See example above for the format.

methods.name: toString
methods.comment: Only give providers, not models.

name: BrowserApp
comment:
Base class for client (browser) applications.
	 Use of this class is optional.

methods.name: navigateTo:
methods.comment: Navigate to url page, preserving test mode.

methods.name: url
methods.comment: Return url of this app.

name: Component
comment:
Base class for client (browser) components.
Instances can be thought of as lightweight React or Angular components.
A component has an HTML fragment, that may include a CSS file,
that can be dynamically loaded into the current page.

For communication between components:
 	Var 'app' optionally holds the (root) BrowserApp instance.
 	Var 'parent' optionally holds the parent Component instance.

methods.name: htmlPath
methods.comment: Answer the (relative) path of this components HTML file.

methods.name: start
methods.comment: Optional startup code that is called after loading self.

methods.name: loadIntoElement:append:
methods.comment: Load self into the HTML element with argument id.

name: Language
comment:
Manages the displayed language for the user.
It works by replacing text in HTML elements using their id's.
The translated languages are loaded JSON files with an array of <id>, <text>.
The JSON file should be named '<language>.json' and placed in the './Language' directory.
The preferred language is detected through 'Window navigator language'.
HTML can retain static text in the default language, for readability and testing.

classMethods.name: supported:
classMethods.comment:
Create a new langauge object with an array of supported language id's by this site.
	 The first language is used as the default language.

methods.name: currentIndex
methods.comment: Return index of current language in supported languages.

methods.name: load:
methods.comment: Load argument language code into the current HTML page.

methods.name: matchCode:
methods.comment:
Match the argument language ID to the closest language supported.
	 The code should be in the ISO format '<language>[-<country>], e.g. 'en-US'.
	 The country is optional and the fallback is first supported equal language from another country.

name: LanguageCode
comment:
THis class encapsulates an ISO language codes in format '<language>[-<country>], e.g.: 'en-US'.
	 It has functionality for (partial) matching with other language codes.

methods.name: fromCode:
methods.comment: Parse argument ISO language code into self.

methods.name: match:
methods.comment: Return true if the argument code matches self, language and country.

methods.name: matchLanguage:
methods.comment: Return true if the argument code language matches self.

methods.name: superclass:
methods.comment: Should only be called by compiler generated code.

methods.name: canUnderstand:
methods.comment: Return true if this or a superclass implement the argument method.

name: Console
comment: This class encasulates the JS class: Console.

name: JsObject
comment:
This abstract class implements and association of a ST object to a JS object.
	 The variable 'js' contains the JS object associated with the ST object.

methods.comment: JsObjects instances are considered equal if their encapsulated JS objects are equal.

methods.name: js
methods.comment: Note: This returns a JS object, not a ST object.

methods.name: js:
methods.comment: Note: aJs should be a JS object, not a ST object.

methods.name: atJsProperty:
methods.comment:
Returns ST object for JS property value in current object.
	 An instance of: String, Number subclass, Boolean, Nil.

methods.name: atJsProperty:put:
methods.comment: Put the .js of argument in self.

methods.name: isNull
methods.comment: Also returns true if this.js is 'undefined'.

methods.name: isUndefined
methods.comment: Also returns true if this.js is 'null'.

methods.name: toJs
methods.comment:
Returns js representation of self.
	 Array overloads this to convert array elements to JS also, recursively.

name: SqlObject
comment:
This class implements an abstract SQL Object.
	 It is used for leightweight ORM and CRUD operations.
	 The variable 'id' contains  the unique integer that must also be a serial (autonumber) column in the associated SQL table.
	 This base class is designed to be independent of other database classes,
	 so it can be used in the front-end (browser) as well as in the back-end (Node.JS).
 	 Note: Using this base class for SQL database access is optional.

classMethods.name: fromRows:
classMethods.comment: Return array of instances of self (subclass) with data from Object class rows.

classMethods.name: fromRow:
classMethods.comment: Return instance of self (subclass) with data from row.

methods.name: columnValues
methods.comment: Return array with values of columns variables, excluding the id column.

name: Test
comment:
Base class for all unit test classes.
	 Class method testAll calls all test* methods in subclasses.
	 test* methods should call assert: [...] with the block returning the result as a boolean expression of a specific test.
	 Example: 'self assert: [ 2 < 3 ]'
	 The tester throws an error: if a test fails and stops testing.

methods.name: disabled
methods.comment: Override this to conditionally disable tests.

methods.name: all
methods.comment: Call all methods named 'test*' on self for tests not disabled.

methods.name: assertError:
methods.comment: Evalute block expecting an error. If *no* error occurs, then raise an error.

methods.name: timeout:
methods.comment:
Promise version of timeout.
	 This enables awaiting small delays (sleep).
	 This function rettuns JS undefined, so that cannot be used in ST.

name: Dictionary
comment:
Dictionary that can have arbitrary objects as keys.
Note: Simple, low performance implementation.
Use class Map when keys are strings.

classMethods.name: fromArray:
classMethods.comment:
Create dictionary from array of arrays,
	where the inner arrays have two elements: key and value.

methods.name: toArray
methods.comment:
Convert self to array of arrays,
	where the inner arrays have two elements: key and value.

methods.name: find:
methods.comment: Return KeyValue with key or nil if not found.

methods.name: at:
methods.comment: Return value at key, or thorw an error if the key is absent.

methods.name: at:ifAbsent:
methods.comment: Return value at key, or the results of block if the key is absent.

methods.name: do:
methods.comment: Evaluate block with each key and value pair as argument.

name: KeyValue
comment:
Simple holder for a key and value pair.
Used in class Dictionary.

name: Crypto
comment: This class also implements Node.js module: crypto.subtle.

classMethods.name: verify:key:signature:data:
classMethods.header: async verify: algorithm key: key signature: signature data: data

classMethods.name: deriveKey:key:type:extractable:usages:
classMethods.header: async deriveKey: algorithm key: key type: type extractable: extractable usages: usages

name: EventId
comment:
Implement an association between ST and JS event blocks,
	 needed when removing event listeners.

classMethods.name: fromType:block:jsBlock:
classMethods.header: fromType: type block: block jsBlock: jsBlock

name: Fetch
comment: Implements JS fetch() API plus some conversions.

classMethods.name: text:options:
classMethods.comment: Returns the fetched file content as a text string.

classMethods.name: object:options:
classMethods.comment: Returns a ST object constructed from the the fetched JSON response.

name: Point
comment: Holds 2 ST numbers x and y. Arithmatic can be done the point as a whole this.

methods.name: z
methods.comment: Assume 2d poin is at z = 0 in 3D plane.

methods.category:
These larger-than operators cannot be inherited
because a < b does not always imply b > a

These larger-than operators cannot be inherited
because a < b does not always imply b > a

Basic arithmatic with magnitude (number or point) subsclass arguments.
 Delegates to point handling methods of argument.

Basic arithmatic with magnitude (number or point) subsclass arguments.

Basic arithmatic with magnitude (number or point) subsclass arguments.

Basic arithmatic with magnitude (number or point) subsclass arguments.

methods.category: Basic arithmatic with large integer arguments.

methods.category: Basic arithmatic with large integer arguments.

methods.category: Basic arithmatic with large integer arguments.

name: Rect
comment:
Implements a rectangle at point origin and with and hight in point extent.
	 Note coordinate system assumes origin at screen top-left with x positive to the right and y positive downwards.

classMethods.name: fromJs:
classMethods.comment: Note: The argument should be a DOMRect, which is not available in Node.js.

name: SqlTable
comment:
This class implements an abstract SQL Table.
The table in the database should have an column named 'id' of type autonumber to manage peristance.
Variable rowClass should refer to a subsclass of SqlObject, of which instances map to rows in this table.

classMethods.name: database:name:rowClass:
classMethods.header: database: database name: name rowClass: rowClass

methods.name: quote
methods.comment: Return character used for quoting table and column names as a strings.

methods.name: quoted:
methods.comment: Return argument name quoted using database quoting convention.

methods.name: quotedName
methods.comment: Return table name quoted.

methods.name: columnList
methods.comment: Return my columns as comma separated list excluding the id column.

methods.name: columnListWithId
methods.comment: Return my columns as comma separated list

methods.name: parameter:
methods.comment: Return a SQL string for the parameter numbered by index

methods.name: insertValuesParameters
methods.comment:
Return a string suitable for e.g. the VALUES clause of an INSERT statement when using parameters, excluding the id column.
	 E.g.: '$1, $2, $3' for a three column table, excluding the id column.

methods.name: updateSetParameters
methods.comment:
Return a string suitable for e.g. the SET clause of an UPDATE argument when using parameters, excluding the id column.
	 E.g.: ' col1 = $1, col2 = $2, col3 = $3' for a three column table, excluding the id column, but using quoted column names.

methods.name: columnValues:
methods.comment:
Return array with values of columns variables, excluding the id column.
	 This method may be overloaded with database specific value converions needed,
	 depending on the type support of that database.

name: Fs
comment:
This class only implements the *sync* API of the fs module.
 So the callback API is not implemented.
 Use the promises based class Fsp for async.

name: Os
comment: Use the second import form, because it's compatible with NW.js.

classMethods.name: networkInterfaces
classMethods.comment:
Returns a map where each keys is an interface name
	 and each value is and array of associated OsNetworkInterfaceInfo objects
	 with differen addresses.

name: OsConstants
comment:
Constant values can be retrieved by calling: atJsProperty: '<constant name>'
 on the objects returned from methods 'errno', 'signals' and 'priority'.
 Constant names can be found here:
	https://nodejs.org/api/os.html#osconstants

name: Process
comment: Environment 'env' functionality is implemented in the Environment class.

classMethods.name: hasPermission:reference:
classMethods.comment: Note: Node must be started with the --permission flag to enable this.

name: Ai
comment:
Base class with functionality common to all AI providers.
 Specific AI providers extend this class.

classMethods.name: providers
classMethods.comment:
Return available AI providers as an instance of AiProviders,
	 loaded from environment variables.

classMethods.name: baseUrl
classMethods.comment: Return base URL key set in varable <classname>_BASE_URL, e.g.: OPENAI_BASE_URL

classMethods.name: models
classMethods.comment: Return list of model names set in varable <classname>_MODELS, e.g.: OPENAI_MODELS

classMethods.name: apiKey
classMethods.comment: Return API key set in varable <classname>_API_KEY, e.g.: OPENAI_API_KEY

classMethods.name: available
classMethods.comment: Return true if the base URL key is set for this AI.

classMethods.name: available:
classMethods.comment: Return true if the API key is set for the named AI.

methods.name: chat:
methods.comment:
Ask a question to this AI provider with the text strings in the messages array,
	 and call the block with the response text.

methods.name: nextId
methods.comment: Generate unique ID to use for differentiating AI responses.

This evaluates block with a ContentResponse.

name: Selection
comment: This class encasulates the JS class Range.

classMethods.name: new
classMethods.comment:
2024-06-16: FireFox fails to get selected text through the global document.getSelection() call.
	 This is the official bug report: https://bugzilla.mozilla.org/show_bug.cgi?id=85686
	 The workaround is to extract the selection through selectionStart and selectionEnd
	 of a specific input field.

name: CanvasGradient
comment:
From MDN:
 The CanvasGradient interface represents an opaque object describing a gradient.
 It is returned by one of these methods from the class CanvasRenderingContext2D:
 createLinearGradient(), createConicGradient() or createRadialGradient()

name: CanvasPattern
comment:
From MDN:
 The CanvasPattern interface represents an opaque object
 describing a pattern, based on an image, a canvas, or a video,
 created by the CanvasRenderingContext2D.createPattern() method.

This class also implements DomMatrixReadOnly.

Data

The value instance variable is always JS null, for convenience in calling the JS API.

name: Map
comment:
Encapsulates the JS Map class, that is stored in the inherited 'js' variable.
	 As in a JS Map, the keys must be strings.
	 Otherwise use class Dictionary.

Create map from array of arrays,
	where the inner arrays have two elements: key and value.

This class implements an abstract SQL Table.
Variable rowClass should refer to a subsclass of SqlObject,
of which instances map to rows in this table.

Insert sqlObject as a new row in self.

name: PostgresTable
comment:
This class implements an abstract SQL Table.
	 Variable rowClass should refer to a subsclass of SqlObject, of which instances map to rows in this table.

methods.name: quote
methods.comment: Return character used for quoting table and column names as a strings.

methods.name: parameter:
methods.comment: Return a SQL srign for the paramater numbered by index

methods.name: selectId:

methods.name: insert:
methods.comment:
Insert sqlObject as a new row in self.
	 The id variable will be set to a new serial value after the insert.
	 If the insert fails, the id will be (re)set to nil.

methods.comment: Delete all rows from table.

name: SqliteTable

This class implements an abstract SQL Table.
 Variable rowClass should refer to a subsclass of SqlObject,
 of which instances map to rows in this table,
 using an obigatory autonumber id column.

methods.name: parameter:

	 with conversions needed for SQLite: Date, Boolean.

methods.name: selectId:
methods.comment: Return single SqlObject from row with argument id, otherwise nil.

methods.name: insert:
methods.comment:
Insert sqlObject as a new row in self.
	 The id variable will be set to a new serial value after the insert.

methods.comment: Update sqlObject in table using id.

methods.name: delete:
methods.comment: Delete sqlObject in table using id.
